sanskrit: आवृतं ज्ञानमेतेन ज्ञानिनो नित्यवैरिणा । कामरूपेण कौन्तेय दुष्पूरेणानलेन च ॥
hindiTranslation: और हे कुन्तीनन्दन  इस अग्निके समान कभी तृप्त न होनेवाले और विवेकियोंके नित्य वैरी इस कामके द्वारा मनुष्यका विवेक ढका हुआ है ॥
englishTranslation: O son of Kunti, wisdom is covered by this desire, the eternal enemy of the wise, as fire is never satisfied.
essence: Desire does not just tempt; it hides wisdom itself.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Desire hides clear understanding. It never feels satisfied, and it keeps working against the wise like an unquenchable fire.
simpleInsight: You can see how craving does not just tempt you; it blinds you.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen while Krishna explains the real enemy behind confusion. Desire does not merely distract; it covers wisdom itself, like a fire that never says enough.
modernRelevance: You open one more tab, then another, then another, hoping the urge will settle. It does not. It keeps your judgment fogged until you notice what is driving you.
translationLiteral: आवृतम् (covered) / ज्ञानम् (wisdom) / एतेन (by this) / ज्ञानिनः (of the wise) / नित्य-वैरिणा (by the eternal enemy) / काम-रूपेण (in the form of desire) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / दुष्पूरेण (by the hard-to-satisfy) / अनलेन (by fire)
